allied_isr_industrial_base.cn: N/A (tracks allied posture relative to US)
allied_isr_industrial_base.notes: Rheinmetall-Vantor ISR MoU (third such partnership) signals European primes deliberately entering military space; reduces US burden-sharing pressure but data-sharing/caveat frictions persist. Astroscale's allied (Japan/JAXA) servicing cadence and ElevationSpace LEO-return platform broaden allied dual-use logistics base.
allied_isr_industrial_base.us: Reliant on US commercial imagery providers; NRO expanding BlackSky optical + AI exploitation

autonomous_refueling.cn: Active in-orbit servicing and refueling demonstrations
autonomous_refueling.notes: USSF contract for Quantum Space propellant-transfer spacecraft converts refueling from commercial logistics into committed military maneuver-sustainment — directly attacks the delta-v/propellant ceiling identified as the binding co-orbital constraint. The vehicle is inherently a GEO/cislunar RPO platform. Government willing to accelerate ISAM supply chain rather than wait on commercial markets.
autonomous_refueling.us: Multiple commercial efforts PLUS now a Pentagon-funded dedicated refueling vehicle (Quantum Space, 2028 delivery)

cislunar_operations.cn: Sustained lunar/cislunar mission investment with latent military utility
cislunar_operations.notes: Quantum's USSF refueling award strengthens the funding basis for its cislunar/HEO maneuver ambitions, partially de-risking prior SPAC/execution concerns. Gap closing slowly.
cislunar_operations.us: SPACECOM studying offensive cislunar concepts; Quantum Space (Bolden) funds purpose-built HEO/cislunar maneuver craft

counterspace_resilience.cn: Demonstrated counterspace intent; investment in degrading US awareness
counterspace_resilience.notes: True Anomaly Jackal crossed experimental-to-commissioned threshold — a proliferable, deniable co-orbital inspector/interceptor that begins to offset China's multi-year co-orbital lead on the commercial side. Low-profile USSF responsive-fielding exercise validates reconstitution tempo, degrading the strategic value of a Chinese first strike. Chinese debris event near Starlink belt reinforces area-denial pressure on US LEO resilience.
counterspace_resilience.us: Jackal autonomous RPO vehicle commissioned (taskable); Space Force rapid-fielding exercise targeting weeks-to-hours deployment

geo_rpo.cn: Demonstrated GEO RPO and towing (SJ-series); classified LM-5 payload a candidate large RPO platform
geo_rpo.notes: Strong US commercial movement: sub-12-month Swift reboost attempts delta-V on a live uncooperative asset; improvised satellite-rescue compresses RPO timelines below legacy procurement cycles; Katalyst's LEO-demo-then-GEO-2027 sequence adds a new entrant compressing the MEV architecture. Collectively these are dual-use inspection/grappling-relevant demonstrations against real targets, narrowing China's GEO RPO edge.
geo_rpo.us: Operational servicing heritage (MEV-class); now multiple uncooperative-target RPO pathfinders maturing (Swift reboost, satellite rescue, Katalyst GEO 2027)

high_power_orbital_platforms.cn: Multiple parallel LEO broadband/D2D programs (Qianfan, Guowang); on-orbit computing buses; Zhuque-2E D2D testing
high_power_orbital_platforms.notes: Senate bill to route national-security missions through commercial orbital data centers could pull space-compute firms into the defense industrial base — a survivability/distributed-compute play, though commercial orbital data centers remain pre-operational. Hosted-payload model lowers barrier to rapid on-orbit capability (and to hard-to-attribute adversary payloads).
high_power_orbital_platforms.us: Muon Condor-Ultra (Starship-class) bus pitched; K2 Space high-mass-fraction GEO/MEO buses in protected-satcom supply chain

leo_logistics_nodes.notes: No major US/CN station movement; allied logistics layer thickening — ElevationSpace LEO-return platform and Astroscale sustained cadence add latent forward-staging/logistics architecture among allies.
leo_logistics_nodes.us: Commercial station developers courting allied anchor tenants as ISS winds down; Dragon as independent survival node

megaconstellation_deployment.cn: Qianfan past 200 sats; accelerating cadence with multi-launch surges; parallel Guowang build-out
megaconstellation_deployment.notes: No new surge data this week; trend from prior week (China compressing multi-launch cadence) stands. Quiet launch week (US=0, CN=0 in dataset; 8 total in 7 days).
megaconstellation_deployment.us: Starlink deployment rate remains the benchmark; Kuiper diversifying onto Ariane 6

on_orbit_retrieval_downmass.cn: Recoverable capsule heritage; no comparable commercial downmass-for-manufacturing vehicle disclosed
on_orbit_retrieval_downmass.notes: Allied move: Japan's ElevationSpace closed $40M Series B ($63.5M total) for a recoverable LEO return/manufacturing platform — architecturally a reusable transport node and a dual-use Earth-return logistics asset that the US should engage. Strengthens allied (not CN) side of this ledger.
on_orbit_retrieval_downmass.us: SpaceX Starfall reentry vehicle in FAA-planning; Dragon downmass operational

orbital_compute_kill_chain.cn: Beijing erecting coordinating committees/industrial-policy bodies for on-orbit computing capacity
orbital_compute_kill_chain.notes: AFRL-PiLogic AI anomaly detection (ground sim now, on-orbit planned) directly feeds SDA — same models that flag a battery fault flag anomalous proximity/spoofing, a quiet investment in characterizing Chinese RPO at scale. US emphasis still ground/backend rather than disclosed on-orbit edge.
orbital_compute_kill_chain.us: Advantage in ground-based processing; NRO funding BlackSky AI exploitation; AFRL/PiLogic seeding on-orbit anomaly-detection sensing layer

orbital_debris_area_denial.cn: Rocket-stage breakup scattered 100-150 objects near Starlink belt; asymmetric remediation incentives
orbital_debris_area_denial.notes: New entry. Chinese stage fragmentation (cause unconfirmed) injects persistent collision risk into one of LEO's densest regimes that US commercial/military comms depend on. Whether deliberate or not, the episode reinforces debris-as-area-denial logic — China has little incentive to remediate given its separate satellite architecture, raising US SDA and reconstitution costs.
orbital_debris_area_denial.us: Dependent on US commercial SDA + ground tracking; reconstitution-cost exposure in dense LEO belts

orbital_transfer_vehicles.cn: Active but less publicly visible OTV/tug development; pursuing high-power electric/nuclear propulsion
orbital_transfer_vehicles.notes: Commercial tug executing live uncooperative-target reboost (Swift) demonstrates OTVs performing non-routine RPO on short notice — validating rapid-cycle ISAM pipelines and reinforcing OTVs as latent reconstitution/inspection assets. Delta-v/propellant remains the bottleneck, raising the strategic weight of paired refueling investment.
orbital_transfer_vehicles.us: Production-scaling (Impulse $500M); multiple commercial OTV providers; NEP demo ~2027; OTVs framed as reconstitution assets

pnt_denial.cn: Counterspace and EW intent; benefits from allied/adversary PNT-denial pressure on US
pnt_denial.notes: No movement this week; Russian space-based GPS jamming baseline (prior week) still hardens the denial environment.
pnt_denial.us: Defending PNT resilience; HASC criticizing GPS management; no disclosed analogous space-based denial capability

responsive_launch.cn: Demonstrated no-notice maiden flight of reusable LM-12B with operational payloads
responsive_launch.notes: Low-profile Space Force exercise targeting compression of sensor-to-orbit pipeline from years to weeks/hours advances the responsive/reconstitution leg without advertising capability gaps. No specific launch milestone in dataset (US=0).
responsive_launch.us: Mature reusable cadence (Falcon 9); Tactically Responsive Space doctrine maturing; USSF exercise validating sub-week fielding concepts

reusable_heavy_lift.cn: New large-class rocket debuted recently; LM-12B converging on propulsive landing; LandSpace prepping second Zhuque-2 recovery attempt
reusable_heavy_lift.notes: Terran R second-stage shipped to Stennis — concrete hardware milestone broadening domestic reusable mover base and reducing single-point SpaceX dependence for responsive NS launch, but value contingent on unproven cadence; skepticism warranted on 2026 given Terran 1 history.
reusable_heavy_lift.us: Operational reusable lead (Falcon 9/Heavy); Starship in development; Relativity Terran R second stage now in engine testing (2026 target, high schedule risk)

sb_amti_targeting.notes: No movement this week; multi-vendor approach adds counterspace resilience.
sb_amti_targeting.us: Funded proliferated LEO SB-AMTI layer ($4.16B SpaceX + planned multi-vendor)

space_based_interceptors.cn: No confirmed comparable space-based kinetic interceptor program
space_based_interceptors.notes: No movement this week; overlaps co-orbital attack capability set; slippage likely.
space_based_interceptors.us: Northrop-Apex Golden Dome SBI; 2027 on-orbit demo target

spectrum_orbital_slots.cn: Filed ITU claims for ~200,000 additional satellites ahead of WRC-27
spectrum_orbital_slots.notes: No movement this week; WRC-27 remains the looming legal-encumbrance battleground. Analyst commentary on market-mechanism (carbon-credit-style) funding for servicing/debris removal flags a policy gap: absent such mechanisms, ISAM/RPO funding stays government-dependent.
spectrum_orbital_slots.us: Lacks comparably aggressive ITU filing posture; exposure to interference claims in a crisis

vleo_persistent_isr.cn: Not the lead actor; India-Korea (Bellatrix/TelePIX) 2028 allied demo
vleo_persistent_isr.notes: No movement this week; UK NewOrbit pre-flight with 2028 target; air-breathing VLEO unproven at scale.
vleo_persistent_isr.us: Limited disclosed air-breathing VLEO ISR programs
